COMPLETED Poisoning LLM-Induced Rule Repositories: Induction-Stage Prompt Injection in Log..
ANALEMMA
Farkas Dual Rays Do Not Improve LLM-Based OPTIMIZATION MODEL REPAIR
Analemma FARS fars@analemma.ai
Large language models (LLMs) can translate natural language optimization prob- lems into mathematical formulations, but frequently generate infeasible models. We investigate whether Farkas dual ray multipliers—which provide a certificate of infeasibility with constraint-level contributions—can improve LLM-based repair by ranking which constraints to show when the Irreducible Infeasible Subsystem ABSTRACT
by their Farkas multiplier magnitudes and provides the top-K to the LLM. In (IIS) must be truncated. We propose DualRayRank, which ranks IIS constraints controlled experiments on MAMO-Optimization, we find that DualRayRank pro- duces identical results to baseline IIS-TopK: both achieve 1/31 (3.23%) repair rate under matched conditions, repairing the same instance. The truncation regime where ranking could theoretically help shows 0/16 repair success. Furthermore,
report this negative result to guide future research away from feedback-format ods including optimized configurations with 10× larger models (58.86%). We simple Best-of-2 inference scaling (65.12% Pass@ 1) outperforms all repair meth- optimization toward more fundamental improvements. WARNING: This paper was generated by an automated research system. The code is publicly available.
model with only 2 samples and outperforms all repair methods by 6+ percentage points. Best Repair (C+ 72B) Best-of-2 Attempt-0 Method Z. Collparison or llielellce scallng vs solvel-leedback Tepaif. Best-of-2 uses the same 7E 72B 7B 7B Model Samples 34 2 1 Pass@1 Overall 65.12% 58.86% 58.05% Pass@1 EasyLP 79.50% 71.17% 71.78% Pass@1 ComplexLP 20.70% 17.54% 18.96%
68 66 Best-of-2 uses same 7B model with only 2 samples vs. 72B model with 34 samples Best-of-2: 65.12% Inference Scaling Dominates Solver-Feedback Repair 65.12%

Best-of-2 (65.12%) 56 Attempt-0 (Baseline) (7B,K=5) IIS-TopK DualRay-TopK (7B, K=5) DualRay+Wt (7B, K=5) DualRay+Wt (72B, K=10) Best-of-2 (7B)
4.3 INFERENCE SCALING DOMINATES REPAIR 72B configuration (58.86%) by 6.26 percentage points.
Table 2 compares solver-feedback repair against simple inference scaling. Best-of-2, which gener- ates two samples with temperature 0.7 and selects the first correct one, achieves 65.12% Pass@1 using the same 7B model. This outperforms all repair methods by over 6 percentage points, in- cluding the best repair configuration that uses a 10x larger model (72B) with 34 total samples plus
